summary: A single-story rectangular bunker or fortified structure built partially underground with a concrete foundation and perimeter wall. The main structure features a lower level with sandstone walls and multiple window openings, a middle concrete band, and an upper level with brick cladding topped by a flat roof with concrete coping. The building is surrounded by a white concrete retaining wall and sits in an excavated pit.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box, partially submerged in ground
shape.symmetry: bilateral symmetry along length axis; asymmetrical window placement on visible faces

materials.name: concrete
materials.role: foundation, retaining wall, roof coping, structural bands

elements: multiple rectangular windows (4-6 visible per long side)
concrete foundation band separating sandstone and brick
no visible entrance from exterior views
rebuild_hints: Excavate rectangular pit approximately 12×16 blocks, depth ~4 blocks below ground level
Build concrete foundation floor and outer retaining wall (white concrete blocks)
Construct lower walls from sandstone blocks
Add concrete band/trim at mid-height as structural division
Build upper walls from red brick blocks
Install rectangular window openings: approximately 2 blocks wide × 2 blocks tall, spaced evenly (roughly 3 blocks apart) on long sides
Cap roof with flat concrete blocks and concrete coping trim
Ensure bilateral symmetry along the length axis
Windows appear to be on lower sandstone section primarily, with some on brick section
uncertainties: Exact window count and precise spacing uncertain from angled views
Interior layout and floor plan completely unknown
Entrance location not visible in any exterior view
Exact material for lower walls could be sandstone or stone_brick variant
Roof interior structure (slabs, support) not visible
Depth dimension estimated from perspective; could range 14-18 blocks
Whether structure is fully underground or partially above-ground unclear from ground-level perspective
